units.text: 4. Love is patient and is kind. Love doesn’t envy. Love doesn’t brag, is not proud, 5. doesn’t behave itself inappropriately, doesn’t seek its own way, is not provoked, takes no account of evil; 6. doesn’t rejoice in unrighteousness, but rejoices with the truth; 7. bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, and endures all things.
units.normalizedText: 4 love is patient and is kind love doesn't envy love doesn't brag is not proud 5 doesn't behave itself inappropriately doesn't seek its own way is not provoked takes no account of evil 6 doesn't rejoice in unrighteousness but rejoices with the truth 7 bears all things believes all things hopes all things and endures all things
units.summary: Love is described through patient, truthful, enduring action rather than mere sentiment.
questionsAnswered: How should I treat another person?

units.text: 7. Beloved, let’s love one another, for love is of God; and everyone who loves has been born of God and knows God. 8. He who doesn’t love doesn’t know God, for God is love. 9. By this God’s love was revealed in us, that God has sent his only born Son into the world that we might live through him. 10. In this is love, not that we loved God, but that he loved us, and sent his Son as the atoning sacrifice for our sins. 11. Beloved, if God loved us in this way, we also ought to love one another. 12. No one has seen God at any time. If we love one another, God remains in us, and his love has been perfected in us.
units.normalizedText: 7 beloved let's love one another for love is of god and everyone who loves has been born of god and knows god 8 he who doesn't love doesn't know god for god is love 9 by this god's love was revealed in us that god has sent his only born son into the world that we might live through him 10 in this is love not that we loved god but that he loved us and sent his son as the atoning sacrifice for our sins 11 beloved if god loved us in this way we also ought to love one another 12 no one has seen god at any time if we love one another god remains in us and his love has been perfected in us
units.summary: God is love, shown in sending his Son, and God's love is made visible as believers love one another.

units.text: 16. Every Scripture is God-breathed and profitable for teaching, for reproof, for correction, and for instruction in righteousness, 17. that each person who belongs to God may be complete, thoroughly equipped for every good work.
units.normalizedText: 16 every scripture is god-breathed and profitable for teaching for reproof for correction and for instruction in righteousness 17 that each person who belongs to god may be complete thoroughly equipped for every good work
units.summary: Scripture is God-breathed and forms people for every good work.
questionsAnswered: How does the Bible form a person?

units.text: 15. He is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn of all creation. 16. For by him all things were created in the heavens and on the earth, visible things and invisible things, whether thrones or dominions or principalities or powers. All things have been created through him and for him. 17. He is before all things, and in him all things are held together. 18. He is the head of the body, the assembly, who is the beginning, the firstborn from the dead, that in all things he might have the preeminence. 19. For all the fullness was pleased to dwell in him, 20. and through him to reconcile all things to himself by him, whether things on the earth or things in the heavens, having made peace through the blood of his cross.
units.normalizedText: 15 he is the image of the invisible god the firstborn of all creation 16 for by him all things were created in the heavens and on the earth visible things and invisible things whether thrones or dominions or principalities or powers all things have been created through him and for him 17 he is before all things and in him all things are held together 18 he is the head of the body the assembly who is the beginning the firstborn from the dead that in all things he might have the preeminence 19 for all the fullness was pleased to dwell in him 20 and through him to reconcile all things to himself by him whether things on the earth or things in the heavens having made peace through the blood of his cross
units.summary: Jesus is the image of the invisible God, supreme over creation, and the one through whom all things hold together and peace is made.
questionsAnswered: Why does Jesus matter for all creation?

units.text: 8. for by grace you have been saved through faith, and that not of yourselves; it is the gift of God, 9. not of works, that no one would boast. 10. For we are his workmanship, created in Christ Jesus for good works, which God prepared before that we would walk in them.
units.normalizedText: 8 for by grace you have been saved through faith and that not of yourselves it is the gift of god 9 not of works that no one would boast 10 for we are his workmanship created in christ jesus for good works which god prepared before that we would walk in them
units.summary: Salvation is by grace through faith, and God's people are created in Christ for good works.

units.text: 31. Let all bitterness, wrath, anger, outcry, and slander be put away from you, with all malice. 32. And be kind to one another, tender hearted, forgiving each other, just as God also in Christ forgave you.
units.normalizedText: 31 let all bitterness wrath anger outcry and slander be put away from you with all malice 32 and be kind to one another tender hearted forgiving each other just as god also in christ forgave you
units.summary: Bitterness and malice are put away as believers forgive one another as God forgave them in Christ.
questionsAnswered: How does God's forgiveness shape mine?

units.text: 14. God said to Moses, “I AM WHO I AM,” and he said, “You shall tell the children of Israel this: ‘I AM has sent me to you.’” 15. God said moreover to Moses, “You shall tell the children of Israel this, ‘Yahweh, the God of your fathers, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob, has sent me to you.’ This is my name forever, and this is my memorial to all generations.
units.normalizedText: 14 god said to moses i am who i am and he said you shall tell the children of israel this 'i am has sent me to you ' 15 god said moreover to moses you shall tell the children of israel this 'yahweh the god of your fathers the god of abraham the god of isaac and the god of jacob has sent me to you ' this is my name forever and this is my memorial to all generations
units.summary: God names himself as I AM and identifies himself with the covenant God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
questionsAnswered: What does God reveal about himself?

units.text: 1. In the beginning, God created the heavens and the earth.
units.normalizedText: 1 in the beginning god created the heavens and the earth
units.summary: The Bible opens by naming God as creator of the heavens and the earth.

units.text: 26. God said, “Let’s make man in our image, after our likeness. Let them have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the birds of the sky, and over the livestock, and over all the earth, and over every creeping thing that creeps on the earth.” 27. God created man in his own image. In God’s image he created him; male and female he created them. 28. God blessed them. God said to them, “Be fruitful, multiply, fill the earth, and subdue it. Have dominion over the fish of the sea, over the birds of the sky, and over every living thing that moves on the earth.”
units.normalizedText: 26 god said let's make man in our image after our likeness let them have dominion over the fish of the sea and over the birds of the sky and over the livestock and over all the earth and over every creeping thing that creeps on the earth 27 god created man in his own image in god's image he created him male and female he created them 28 god blessed them god said to them be fruitful multiply fill the earth and subdue it have dominion over the fish of the sea over the birds of the sky and over every living thing that moves on the earth
units.summary: Humanity is made in God's image and given a vocation within creation.

units.text: 1. God, having in the past spoken to the fathers through the prophets at many times and in various ways, 2. has at the end of these days spoken to us by his Son, whom he appointed heir of all things, through whom also he made the worlds. 3. His Son is the radiance of his glory, the very image of his substance, and upholding all things by the word of his power, who, when he had by himself purified us of our sins, sat down on the right hand of the Majesty on high,
units.normalizedText: 1 god having in the past spoken to the fathers through the prophets at many times and in various ways 2 has at the end of these days spoken to us by his son whom he appointed heir of all things through whom also he made the worlds 3 his son is the radiance of his glory the very image of his substance and upholding all things by the word of his power who when he had by himself purified us of our sins sat down on the right hand of the majesty on high
units.summary: The Son is the radiance of God's glory and exact imprint of his nature, sustaining all things and making purification for sins.
questionsAnswered: How does the Son reveal God?

units.text: 1. Now faith is assurance of things hoped for, proof of things not seen. 2. For by this, the elders obtained approval. 3. By faith we understand that the universe has been framed by the word of God, so that what is seen has not been made out of things which are visible.
units.normalizedText: 1 now faith is assurance of things hoped for proof of things not seen 2 for by this the elders obtained approval 3 by faith we understand that the universe has been framed by the word of god so that what is seen has not been made out of things which are visible
units.summary: Faith is assurance and conviction that receives God's unseen word and creation's origin.
questionsAnswered: How does faith relate to the unseen?

units.text: 5. But if any of you lacks wisdom, let him ask of God, who gives to all liberally and without reproach, and it will be given to him.
units.normalizedText: 5 but if any of you lacks wisdom let him ask of god who gives to all liberally and without reproach and it will be given to him
units.summary: Anyone lacking wisdom may ask God, who gives generously.
questionsAnswered: What should I do when I lack wisdom?

units.text: 1. In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God. 2. The same was in the beginning with God. 3. All things were made through him. Without him, nothing was made that has been made. 4. In him was life, and the life was the light of men. 5. The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness hasn’t overcome it. 6. There came a man sent from God, whose name was John. 7. The same came as a witness, that he might testify about the light, that all might believe through him. 8. He was not the light, but was sent that he might testify about the light. 9. The true light that enlightens everyone was coming into the world. 10. He was in the world, and the world was made through him, and the world didn’t recognize him. 11. He came to his own, and those who were his own didn’t receive him. 12. But as many as received him, to them he gave the right to become God’s children, to those who believe in his name: 13. who were born, not of blood, nor of the will of the flesh, nor of the will of man, but of God. 14. The Word became flesh and lived among us. We saw his glory, such glory as of the only born Son of the Father, full of grace and truth.
units.normalizedText: 1 in the beginning was the word and the word was with god and the word was god 2 the same was in the beginning with god 3 all things were made through him without him nothing was made that has been made 4 in him was life and the life was the light of men 5 the light shines in the darkness and the darkness hasn't overcome it 6 there came a man sent from god whose name was john 7 the same came as a witness that he might testify about the light that all might believe through him 8 he was not the light but was sent that he might testify about the light 9 the true light that enlightens everyone was coming into the world 10 he was in the world and the world was made through him and the world didn't recognize him 11 he came to his own and those who were his own didn't receive him 12 but as many as received him to them he gave the right to become god's children to those who believe in his name 13 who were born not of blood nor of the will of the flesh nor of the will of man but of god 14 the word became flesh and lived among us we saw his glory such glory as of the only born son of the father full of grace and truth
units.summary: Jesus is the eternal Word who is with God, is God, and becomes flesh to reveal divine glory and grace.
questionsAnswered: How does God reveal himself in Jesus?

units.text: 6. Jesus said to him, “I am the way, the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father, except through me.
units.normalizedText: 6 jesus said to him i am the way the truth and the life no one comes to the father except through me
units.summary: Jesus identifies himself as the way, the truth, and the life, and the way to the Father.
questionsAnswered: How does one come to the Father?

units.text: 16. For God so loved the world, that he gave his only born Son, that whoever believes in him should not perish, but have eternal life. 17. For God didn’t send his Son into the world to judge the world, but that the world should be saved through him.
units.normalizedText: 16 for god so loved the world that he gave his only born son that whoever believes in him should not perish but have eternal life 17 for god didn't send his son into the world to judge the world but that the world should be saved through him
units.summary: God's love is shown in giving his Son so believers may have eternal life and the world may be saved through him.

units.text: 28. “Come to me, all you who labor and are heavily burdened, and I will give you rest. 29. Take my yoke upon you and learn from me, for I am gentle and humble in heart; and you will find rest for your souls. 30. For my yoke is easy, and my burden is light.”
units.normalizedText: 28 come to me all you who labor and are heavily burdened and i will give you rest 29 take my yoke upon you and learn from me for i am gentle and humble in heart and you will find rest for your souls 30 for my yoke is easy and my burden is light
units.summary: Jesus invites the weary and burdened to come to him for rest under his gentle yoke.
questionsAnswered: Where can the weary find rest?
What does Jesus offer the burdened?

units.text: 37. Jesus said to him, “‘You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your soul, and with all your mind.’ 38. This is the first and great commandment. 39. A second likewise is this, ‘You shall love your neighbor as yourself.’ 40. The whole law and the prophets depend on these two commandments.”
units.normalizedText: 37 jesus said to him 'you shall love the lord your god with all your heart with all your soul and with all your mind ' 38 this is the first and great commandment 39 a second likewise is this 'you shall love your neighbor as yourself ' 40 the whole law and the prophets depend on these two commandments
units.summary: Jesus summarizes the Law and Prophets as love for God and love for neighbor.

units.text: 9. Pray like this: “‘Our Father in heaven, may your name be kept holy. 10. Let your Kingdom come. Let your will be done on earth as it is in heaven. 11. Give us today our daily bread. 12. Forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors. 13. Bring us not into temptation, but deliver us from the evil one. For yours is the Kingdom, the power, and the glory forever. Amen.’
units.normalizedText: 9 pray like this 'our father in heaven may your name be kept holy 10 let your kingdom come let your will be done on earth as it is in heaven 11 give us today our daily bread 12 forgive us our debts as we also forgive our debtors 13 bring us not into temptation but deliver us from the evil one for yours is the kingdom the power and the glory forever amen '
units.summary: Jesus teaches prayer as address to the Father for God's kingdom, daily provision, forgiveness, and deliverance.

units.text: 5. Trust in Yahweh with all your heart, and don’t lean on your own understanding. 6. In all your ways acknowledge him, and he will make your paths straight.
units.normalizedText: 5 trust in yahweh with all your heart and don't lean on your own understanding 6 in all your ways acknowledge him and he will make your paths straight
units.summary: Wisdom calls people to trust the LORD rather than their own understanding and to acknowledge him in their paths.
questionsAnswered: How should I make a decision?

units.text: 18. Yahweh is near to those who have a broken heart, and saves those who have a crushed spirit.
units.normalizedText: 18 yahweh is near to those who have a broken heart and saves those who have a crushed spirit
units.summary: The LORD is near to the brokenhearted and saves the crushed in spirit.
questionsAnswered: Is God near when I am broken?
What comfort is there in suffering?

units.text: 4. For whatever things were written before were written for our learning, that through perseverance and through encouragement of the Scriptures we might have hope.
units.normalizedText: 4 for whatever things were written before were written for our learning that through perseverance and through encouragement of the scriptures we might have hope
units.summary: Scripture was written for instruction so that endurance and encouragement would give hope.
